title: Async spawn with profile, per-child ToolContext, and completion notification
purpose: SpawnAgentTool is currently synchronous (blocks parent), creates a bare LoopContext with no profile, and passes an empty tool definitions slice to the child. This brief rewrites spawn to be async via tokio::spawn, accept a profile parameter for child configuration, build per-child ToolContext (solving the shared identity problem where children thought they were root), pass filtered tool definitions so the child model can see its tools, and send completion notifications to the parent via mailbox. This is the core brief that makes multi-agent coordination work.
task: Create tools/agent/handle.rs with AgentHandle (agent_id, status watch::Receiver, InboundSender, JoinHandle) and AgentHandles collection type. Rewrite SpawnAgentTool::execute to: (1) accept optional profile parameter, resolve via libnorn scanner, build child LoopContext via from_profile, (2) build per-child ToolContext with child-specific AgentToolInfra (correct agent_id, parent_id), (3) extract filtered ToolDefinitions from the parent's ToolRegistry through the allow-list, (4) launch child via tokio::spawn, return immediately with agent_id, (5) tokio::spawn wrapper handles completion: marks registry status, sends mailbox notification with trigger_turn:true, updates status watch channel. Rewrite SubAgentExecutor to own a child_context: Arc<ToolContext> and dispatch tool calls through the full four-phase lifecycle (pre_validate, execute, post_validate, on_success) with the child's context instead of delegating to registry.execute().

requirements.title: AgentHandle and AgentHandles types
requirements.spec: AgentHandle SHALL encapsulate the runtime resources for a spawned child agent, and AgentHandles SHALL be a ToolContext extension storing all handles keyed by agent_id.
acceptance: AgentHandle struct in tools/agent/handle.rs: agent_id (Uuid), status_rx (watch::Receiver<AgentStatus>), inbound_tx (InboundSender), join_handle (JoinHandle<()>)
AgentHandles struct wrapping Arc<Mutex<HashMap<Uuid, AgentHandle>>>
AgentHandles::insert(handle), AgentHandles::get(agent_id) -> Option<&AgentHandle>, AgentHandles::remove(agent_id)
AgentHandles is Send + Sync (for ToolContext extension storage)
Test: insert and retrieve an AgentHandle by UUID

requirements.title: SubAgentExecutor with per-child ToolContext and full lifecycle
requirements.spec: SubAgentExecutor SHALL own a child-specific ToolContext and dispatch tool calls through the full four-phase lifecycle (pre_validate, execute, post_validate, on_success) with the child's context, NOT the parent's shared context.
acceptance: SubAgentExecutor gains field: child_context: Arc<ToolContext>
SubAgentExecutor::new takes child_context as a parameter
execute() looks up Tool from parent registry via registry.get(name)
execute() builds ToolEnvelope from arguments
execute() calls tool.pre_validate(&envelope, &child_context) — blocks on PreValidateOutcome::Block
execute() calls tool.execute(&envelope, &child_context)
execute() calls tool.post_validate(&output, &child_context) — collects errors
execute() calls tool.on_success(&output, &child_context) when output is not error (on_success takes output and context, not envelope)
Does NOT call self.registry.execute() (which would use the parent's context)
Test: child calls spawn_agent, infra_from returns child's AgentToolInfra (not root's)
Test: child tool call runs through pre_validate with child context

requirements.title: Per-child ToolContext construction
requirements.spec: SpawnAgentTool SHALL construct a child-specific ToolContext with correct identity (agent_id, parent_id) and shared infrastructure (task store, tool catalog, mailbox).
acceptance: Child ToolContext constructed with fresh AgentToolInfra: agent_id = child's UUID, parent_id = Some(spawning agent's UUID)
Child ToolContext gets its own AgentHandles (empty — children can spawn grandchildren)
SharedTaskStore shared from parent (same underlying store — tasks are shared by design)
SharedToolCatalog shared from parent (tool discovery is global)
DiagnosticCollector shared from parent
Test: spawned child's AgentToolInfra has correct agent_id and parent_id

requirements.title: Spawn with profile parameter
requirements.spec: SpawnAgentTool SHALL accept an optional profile parameter and build the child's LoopContext via from_profile() when provided.
acceptance: SpawnAgentArgs gains optional profile: Option<String> field
When profile is Some(name): resolve via norn::profile::loader::resolve_profile, build LoopContext via from_profile()
Child LoopContext gets profile-derived system instructions, tool allow-list, reasoning config, prompt commands
When profile is None: build minimal LoopContext with task as system instruction (improved from bare 'You are a sub-agent')
Input schema updated with profile parameter description
Test: spawn with profile, verify child gets profile's system instructions

requirements.title: Filtered tool definitions for child
requirements.spec: The child agent SHALL receive filtered tool definitions from the parent's ToolRegistry through the allow-list, not an empty slice.
acceptance: Spawn code iterates parent's ToolRegistry, calls tool.name(), tool.description(), tool.input_schema() for each available tool
Filters through the allow-list (same one used by SubAgentExecutor for execution gating)
Builds Vec<ToolDefinition> passed as the tools parameter to run_agent_step
When no allow-list specified: all parent tools included (minus spawn_agent itself to prevent infinite recursion — optional safety)
Child model sees tool names and schemas in its context
Test: spawn with tools=["read", "edit"], verify child receives exactly those ToolDefinitions

requirements.title: Async spawn via tokio::spawn
requirements.spec: SpawnAgentTool SHALL launch the child via tokio::spawn and return immediately with the agent_id and registry path.
acceptance: Child's run_agent_step runs inside tokio::spawn, not blocking the parent
SpawnAgentTool::execute returns immediately with JSON: agent_id, path, status: 'active'
The return payload does NOT contain the child's result (it hasn't finished)
tokio::spawn wrapper creates watch channel pair, passes status_tx into the wrapper
InboundChannel pair created: receiver goes to child's run_agent_step, sender stored in AgentHandle
AgentHandle stored in AgentHandles extension on the spawning agent's ToolContext
Test: spawn returns immediately with agent_id, child executes asynchronously

requirements.title: Completion notification via tokio::spawn wrapper
requirements.spec: When a child reaches terminal status, the spawning infrastructure SHALL send a mailbox notification to the parent with trigger_turn: true.
acceptance: tokio::spawn wrapper awaits run_agent_step, then:
On success: marks child Completing then Completed in AgentRegistry
On error: marks child Failed in AgentRegistry
Sends MailboxMessage to parent's inbox: from = child_id, to = parent_id, content = {agent_id, status, result_summary}, trigger_turn = true
Updates status watch channel with terminal status (wakes WaitAgentTool subscribers)
Test: spawn child, child completes, parent mailbox has notification with trigger_turn true

requirements.title: Updated tool description and guidance
requirements.spec: spawn_agent tool description and usage guidance SHALL reflect the async execution model and profile parameter.
acceptance: spawn_agent.description.md describes async behaviour: 'Launches a sub-agent in the background and returns immediately with the agent_id'
spawn_agent.usage.md includes: profile parameter usage, async workflow (spawn, continue work, wait), parallel spawn patterns, anti-pattern warning (don't spawn then immediately wait)
Input schema updated with profile parameter

boundaries: SHALL NOT modify WaitAgentTool or CloseAgentTool — that is NA-007
SHALL NOT implement sub-agent observability (Progress schema) — that is NA-008
SHALL NOT implement GoalTracker integration with sub-agents — deferred per Tom direction
SHALL NOT add multi-provider sub-agent support — children use the same Arc<dyn Provider> as parent
SHALL NOT add hardcoded limits on child agent count (CO1)
SHALL NOT add todo!() or unimplemented!() macros anywhere
verification: spawn_agent returns immediately with agent_id, child runs async — verified by test
Child's AgentToolInfra has correct agent_id and parent_id — verified by test
Child receives filtered tool definitions — verified by test
Profile-based spawn produces correctly configured child — verified by test
Completion notification arrives in parent mailbox — verified by test
SubAgentExecutor dispatches with child context through full lifecycle — verified by test
No file exceeds 500 lines of code (excluding tests, comments, whitespace)
